title: Try to reconstruct the rails
text: A two-rail cipher creates a very simple zig-zag: the letters alternate between the top and bottom rails. Try checking whether alternating letters from the ciphertext produces meaningful text.

title: Try a specific number of rails
text: Write the ciphertext in two rows by placing every other letter on the top rail and the remaining letters on the bottom rail. If the resulting reading order forms smooth, recognisable English, then the key length is most likely 2 rails.
